body_content:
Published by Travelxploria on Sat Jun 14 2025

Himachal Pradesh is the spot in India where people can enjoy snowfall during the winter season. The beauty of the white mountains lures every person to visit Himachal Pradesh with our best Himachal Pradesh tour packages at least once during the season. If you want to watch snowfall in India and want to visit the coldest hill stations in Himachal Pradesh. plan a trip to this region in the winter.

It is the perfect time to enjoy the view of the snow-topped mountains, the chilly winds, the icy lakes, etc. You can also enjoy various activities that can be done only during snowfall. It is important to plan your trip properly if you want to experience the beauty of hill stations during freezing temperatures.

Top 4 coldest hill stations in Himachal Pradesh

There are a few hill stations in Himachal Pradesh that are extremely cold during the winter season. We have listed a few places that you should visit in Himachal Pradesh, starting with the coldest hill station in the region:

Spiti Valley

The Spiti Valley feels like paradise when the temperature drops below freezing point. It is the coldest hill station in Himachal Pradesh. The entire region is covered by a thick blanket of snow in the winter season. The mesmerizing landscape and the natural beauty are sure to capture your entire attention. You can enjoy solitude as this place is far away from the maddening crowd. One can visit the Buddhist monasteries and be familiar with their culture in this valley.

Best Time: May to September.

Top Things: Explore monasteries, visit Chandratal Lake, explore Pin Valley, enjoy Keylong and engage in adventure activities.

Top Places: Kaza, Kibber, Langza, Hikkim, Komic.

Cost: Average cost varies; budget travelers may spend INR 10,000 to 15,000 for a week.

How to Reach: Fly to Bhuntar Airport, reach by road from Manali, Shimla, or Chandigarh, or take a bus from Himachal Pradesh cities.

Rohtang Pass

The Rohtang Pass is popularly known as the door to Spiti Valley and Leh. It is important to note that you cannot visit this place during excessive snowfall. Even if you cannot enter Rohtang Pass, you can enjoy the view of snow-covered terrain and do fun activities. The pass is open to visitors during the summer season when they can plan various interesting activities such as skiing and snowballing.

Best Time: May to November.

Top Things: Scenic drive, snow activities, photography, visit Rahala Falls.

Top Places: Rohtang Pass, Solang Valley, Rahala Falls.

Cost: INR 2,500 to 3,500 per person.

How to Reach: 51 km from Manali, hire a taxi or take a bus. Permit required for private vehicles.

Conclusion:
These are all the cold hill stations in Himachal Pradesh. While Spiti Valley remains the coldest of all, you should also visit the other places to enjoy the scenic beauty and weather. Research a little about the weather before you plan your tour or let the best travel agency in Kolkata assist you. Knowing about the place will help you to plan better.

Like & Follow our social media accounts on Facebook & Instagram to get the latest updates & offers on holiday packages.

Shimla

Shimla is the most popular hill station in Himachal Pradesh. You can not only enjoy the snowfall but also travel to various temples located in the region. One can visit the Jakhoo Temple, Mansa Devi Temple, and Tara Devi Temple. You can trek to these temples and enjoy the beauty of the snow. There are various other tourist destinations and shopping spots that one should know about if one is planning to visit the hill station during the cold months.

Best Time: March to June (summer) and November to February (winter).

Top Things: Stroll along the Ridge, visit Jakhu Temple, take the Toy Train ride, explore Kufri and go trekking.

Top Places: The Mall, Christ Church, Summer Hill, Indian Institute of Advanced Study, Annandale.

Cost: INR 5,000 to 7,000 per person for a 3-day trip.

How to Reach: By road, rail (Kalka), or air (Jubbarhatti Airport).

Manali

We cannot forget about Manali when we are talking about cold places in Himachal Pradesh. It is located in the Kullu Valley, which is also known as the Valley of Gods. People who have visited Manali know that the best time to go there is during the winter season when the temperatures drop massively. You should also attend the Kasol Music Festival which helps people to know about the musical culture and heritage of the region.

Best Time: March to June (summer), December to February (winter).

Top Things: Rohtang Pass, Solang Valley, Hidimba Devi Temple, Old Manali, river rafting.

Top Places: Rohtang Pass, Solang Valley, Hidimba Devi Temple, Manu Temple, Old Manali.

Cost: INR 8,000 to 10,000 per person for a 3-day trip.

How to Reach: By road (NH3), by rail (Chandigarh), or by air (Bhuntar Airport).

Conclusion:

These are all the cold hill stations in Himachal Pradesh. While Spiti Valley remains the coldest of all, you should also visit the other places to enjoy the scenic beauty and weather. Research a little about the weather before you plan your tour or let the best travel agency in Kolkata assist you. Knowing about the place will help you to plan better.
